result:
## Summary

The root cause of near-zero organic traffic: **Lumina pivoted from "InboxZap" (email cleanup) to an AI meeting assistant, but the codebase never caught up.** Fixed and verified via `npm run build` (server at 127.0.0.1:59075 was unresponsive under heavy sandbox load, so I confirmed via the built static HTML output instead):

**Technical fixes**
- `app/layout.tsx` — sitewide `<title>`/description/OG were still "InboxZap — Clean Your Inbox in Minutes"; every indexed page was advertising a dead product. Rewrote to Lumina, added a title template, `metadataBase`, Twitter card, `SoftwareApplication` JSON-LD, and a dynamic OG image (the old one pointed at a nonexistent `/img/og-inboxzap.png`).
- `/features` and `/pricing` — linked from nav and the homepage CTA but had no route files (404s). Built both out.
- Blog — was a client-only component fetching `/api/posts`, which doesn't exist, so it never rendered anything crawlable. Rebuilt as server components with a real `lib/posts.ts` data source and `/blog/[slug]` pages (SSG, per-post metadata, Article JSON-LD).
- `/blog/post-1`…`post-12` — GSC shows Google still has these indexed (thousands of impressions) from the old email-cleanup content; they now 404. Added 301s to `/blog` instead of leaving them dead.
- `app/sitemap.ts` — was missing blog posts, `/features`, `/pricing` entirely.
- `/faq` redirected to `/#faq`, but no `id="faq"` existed on the homepage. Added a real FAQ section + `FAQPage` JSON-LD.

**Content**
- 6 new indexable blog posts, including one specifically for the ~18 clicks/mo still landing on old brand searches (`inboxzap`, `inboxzap app`, `inboxzap review` per GSC) — `inboxzap-is-now-lumina` captures that residual equity instead of losing it.
- Ran the `seo-keywords` skill to build a prioritized cluster/topic map (`.seo/clients/lumina/keywords/`) — `firecrawl` wasn't reachable in this sandbox, so it's grounded in the GSC gap analysis and category knowledge (Otter.ai/Fireflies/Fathom/Grain) rather than live SERP data; flagged that as a caveat for re-verification.

**Top of the backlog** (see `.seo/clients/lumina/reports/keyword-strategy-2026-07-02.md`): competitor-alternative pages (highest revenue potential), Google Meet/Teams note-taking guides to match Zoom coverage, and a proper 2,000+ word `/features` pillar for "ai meeting assistant" itself.
